fixtures.prompt: Use $claude-code-for-codex to map /hooks into Codex and explain how I would inspect hook configuration.
fixtures.notes: Baseline /hooks mapping should remain file-based, not a fake UI parity claim.

fixtures.prompt: Use $claude-code-for-codex to map /permissions into Codex when PreToolUse hooks can still affect the decision.
expected_mapping_contains: Permission explanation plus local config inspection
fixtures.notes: Permission requests with hook mediation should still route through the permissions boundary.

fixtures.prompt: Use $claude-code-for-codex to map /config into Codex for hook-related runtime settings.
fixtures.notes: Config requests should stay file-level and avoid a fake unified settings UI.

fixtures.prompt: Use $claude-code-for-codex to map /plugin into Codex when the plugin changes runtime hooks.
expected_mapping_contains: Codex skills or plugins where available
fixtures.notes: Plugin questions should preserve the runtime-extension boundary.

fixtures.prompt: Use $claude-code-for-codex to map claude plugin install into Codex when I really want reusable runtime behavior with hooks.
fixtures.notes: Install flow should still distinguish skill versus plugin intent.

fixtures.prompt: Use $claude-code-for-codex to map claude plugin update into Codex when hook behavior changed after an update.
fixtures.notes: Update flow should not collapse into a generic marketplace refresh.

fixtures.prompt: Use $claude-code-for-codex to map claude mcp add into Codex when MCP tool output may also be hook-mediated.
fixtures.notes: MCP admin should stay separate from runtime MCP behavior.

fixtures.prompt: Use $claude-code-for-codex to map /reload-plugins into Codex after changing a plugin that contributes hooks.
fixtures.notes: Reload should stay approximate because there is no universal session-plugin reload surface.
